All 10 in-scope implementation refs were inspected via read-only tools.

SCOPE & DISTRIBUTION: The review baseline's 150 units independently count to implementation:102, contract:6, security_audit:10, ui:14, fixture:3, test:9, hermes_evidence:3, claude_review:3, matching the prompt baseline, registry.js deliverable_counts, manifest delivery_counts, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json pack (range RP19.P00.M00.S01–RP19.P01.M05.S20, 71 P00 + 79 P01 = 150). The live cursor shows CP00-582 closed last with next_subphase RP19.P00.M00.S01, confirming CP00-583 is the correct next pack; CP00-584 begins at RP19.P01.M06.S01 as the binding declares.

IMPLEMENTATION INTEGRITY: src/registry.js, service.js, validators.js, and index.js contain only Object.freeze constants and pure functions — no fs/network/DB/runtime in product code. validateClientPortalCoreCp583Coverage validates the registry's hardcoded distribution against the live plan (pack id, risk class, unit count, first/last unit, dup IDs, RP19-only, per-deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, and per-section row titles), and validateClientPortalCoreCp583ScopeContractFoundationDescriptor enforces descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe/no-raw-payload plus deny-cross-tenant, no audit/permission/fixture/hermes leakage, no state writes, and authority-boundary invariants. The contract JSON acts as a golden deepEqual anchor (program, current_pack, no_write_attestation, scope_contract_foundation_descriptor, cp583_requirements, cp583_no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-583). Validation is substantive, not a rubber stamp.

BOUNDARIES: No-write/no-real-data attestations are consistent across registry, service results, manifest (no_*_runtime, no_real_data_loading, unauthorized_data_omitted, no_*_leak all true) and command-evidence. Authority boundary holds everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claude_not_final_approver=true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true, human final approval required and held outside this receipt. Hermes is descriptor-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true) and not embedded in product runtime.

EVIDENCE & POSTURE: Manifest status=implemented_pending_review, production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label, not an enterprise-trust claim. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly not yet present, consistent with the hardened review sequence. Review-evidence integrity checks pass: auth preflight exit 0 with well-formed JSON, summary-only review correctly prohibited for Risk C, no forbidden tools, no omitted active implementation files. The new package test is wired into npm test and rp19:client-portal-core:validate is registered and recorded passing. The unrelated docs/ldip-integration invalid-attempt file and other dirty files are correctly segregated as out-of-scope and were not treated as pack evidence.

No P0–P3 findings.
